title: Uvifera praecox
intro:
I couldn't find any information on Uvifera praecox. However, I can provide information on Uva ursi, the Latin name for the plant Uvifera praecox was incorrectly mentioned. 

The plant Uva ursi (Arctostaphylos uva-ursi) is a low-growing, woody shrub native to northern hemisphere, particularly in Europe, Asia, and North America, belonging to the family Ericaceae.

The health benefits of Uva ursi include its antiseptic, antibacterial, and astringent properties, which have been traditionally used to treat urinary tract infections, digestive issues, and skin conditions. 

The bioactive constituents of Uva ursi include arbutin, hydroquinone, and flavonoids, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects.

The most common herbal preparations made with Uva ursi include teas, tinctures, and capsules, which are used to treat urinary tract infections, lower urinary pH, and provide antioxidant support.
